sourceRows.FindingKind: Spin-charge-family scalar fields and weak-boson couplings
sourceRows.Summary: Discusses several scalar fields replacing the Standard Model Higgs/Yukawa description, including scalar mass eigenstates that differ from fields coupling to Z_m and W_m.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: Relevant scalar/weak-boson source lead, but external to GU and not a fixed observed W/Z/H mass prediction.

sourceRows.FindingKind: Spin-charge-family explanation for Higgs and Yukawa couplings
sourceRows.Summary: States that several scalar fields determine fermion and weak-boson masses and mixing matrices, with weak-doublet and family-triplet transformation behavior.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: Useful for scalar-source diagnosis; still lacks GU-local observed-field extraction, scalar-potential parameters, and GeV normalization.

sourceRows.FindingKind: Spin-charge-family twice-four families, scalars, and gauge fields
sourceRows.Summary: Presents gauge vector and scalar fields emerging from two kinds of fields and relates the scalar fields and mass matrices to Standard Model Yukawa couplings and Higgs.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: Broad model-source lead, but it predicts a multi-scalar external theory rather than GU-local W/Z/H source rows.

sourceRows.FindingKind: Spin-charge-family predictions beyond the Standard Model
sourceRows.Summary: Describes the theory as Kaluza-Klein-like with two spins and predicts several scalar fields with the weak and hypercharge of the Standard Model Higgs.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: Relevant to Higgs-sector alternatives; no direct target-independent observed W/Z/H mass rows are supplied.

sourceRows.Summary: Summarizes the theory as a d=(13+1) Kaluza-Klein-like route with two Clifford-algebra spin-connection fields that explains charges, vector gauge fields, families, and scalar fields observed as Higgs/Yukawa couplings.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: Geometric high-dimensional context, but still not a GU source-lineage artifact for physical W/Z/H mass extraction.

decision: Do not promote W/Z or Higgs physical masses from the spin-charge-family route. It is a serious external high-dimensional Clifford/Kaluza-Klein scalar-sector lead, but it introduces a separate multi-scalar model and does not supply GU-local d=(13+1) embedding, two-Clifford connection map, observed photon/W/Z/H projection rows, independent W/Z source rows, a single observed-Higgs scalar-source envelope, scalar-potential parameters, absolute GeV scale, or unit normalization.
nextRequiredArtifact: A GU-native derivation of the spin-charge-family d=(13+1) or two-Clifford connection structure, or a proof that it is the same local GU source data.
A target-independent scalar potential and mass-matrix diagonalization that identifies observed W, Z, and Higgs states rather than a general multi-scalar sector.
Observed photon/W/Z/H projection rows and physical pole extraction before comparison with measured boson masses.
Independent W and Z source rows with weak-angle, gauge-coupling, and absolute-scale lineage.
A GU Higgs scalar-source envelope and GeV unit normalization before any W/Z/H mass promotion.
